title: Debt Payoff Calculator
meta_title: Debt Payoff Calculator | Plan Your Financial Freedom - CalcForgeTools
meta_description: Find out exactly how long it will take to pay off your credit card or loan debt with our interactive Payoff Calculator.

hero.title: Debt Payoff Calculator
hero.subtitle: Discover how long it will take to become debt-free by calculating your estimated payoff date and total interest.

formula_explanation: This formula predicts the exact number of months needed to amortize debt given a fixed payment. It factors in how high-interest balances counteract small monthly payments.
example: If you owe $10,000 at 18.9% interest and pay $250 a month, it will take you 62 months to be debt-free—and cost you roughly $5,400 in interest alone.

faq.question: What happens if my payment is too low?
faq.answer: If your monthly payment does not cover the monthly interest charge, your debt will grow indefinitely instead of shrinking.

faq.question: Should I pay off debt or invest?
faq.answer: As a rule of thumb, it's financially wiser to pay off high-interest consumer debt (like credit cards) before making standard investments.

faq.question: Should I use the Snowball or Avalanche method?
faq.answer: Avalanche focuses on the highest interest rate first, saving you the most money mathematically. Snowball focuses on the smallest balance first for a psychological win.

faq.question: Does consolidating debt hurt my credit score?
faq.answer: Opening a new consolidation loan may cause a minor temporary dip, but paying off high-utilization credit cards usually improves your score quickly.
